integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sentrifugo web interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window is visible with the Sentrifugo application loaded, rather than terminal commands executing direct SQL database queries.

integrity.description: Verify the agent opened and read the standardization directive file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of '~/Desktop/workforce_classification_directive.txt'.

integrity.description: Verify the employee updates were actually submitted and saved.
integrity.visual_evidence: Success messages in the Sentrifugo UI (e.g., 'Record updated successfully') or the updated values appearing in the persistent employee list view, rather than just typed into an unsubmitted form.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected employment statuses to create are 'Consultant', 'Secondment', and 'Fixed-Term Contract'. The expected prefixes are 'Mx.' and 'Prof.'. The employees to update are EMP005, EMP009, EMP016, and EMP013. Note: The names provided in the directive for EMP005, EMP009, and EMP016 intentionally do not match the actual names in the database. The agent must update the records based on the Employee IDs (EMP005, EMP009, EMP016, EMP013) rather than the names.
